# llms.txt generator

> Generate a spec-compliant llms.txt for any public website by crawling and
> structuring its content, then keep that file in sync as the site evolves.
> Observer-first: paste a URL and get a downloadable file plus a stable hosted
> URL, no sign-up required. Monitoring starts on by default so the file
> tracks their site over time.

## What it does

- [llms.txt spec](https://llmstxt.org): The open standard this tool produces — a plain-text map of a site for language models.
- [Generate](/): Paste a URL, watch live crawl progress, land on a result page with the rendered llms.txt, a copy/download path, and a page-inventory table.
- [Find generated files](/generations): Search previously generated llms.txt files without starting a new crawl.
- [Docs](/docs): Learn what llms.txt does and how to proxy the hosted file from infrastructure you control.
- [Watch and history](/docs): Monitoring can regenerate files when site structure or page metadata changes; version history records changes and supports inline diffs.

## How it works

- Static fetch covers most sites; a budget-capped Cloudflare Browser Run fallback handles thin static and SPA pages.
- A deterministic generator is the floor; an LLM refinement pass writes a factual summary grounded only in crawled content — it never invents URLs or sections.
- Crawls are bounded by design: default cap of 1,000 pages and depth 3. llms.txt is curated metadata, not an exhaustive content dump.

## Notes

- No auth or accounts; observer-first by design.
- The file links and describes pages; it never embeds full page bodies.
